title: Grape jam
excerpt: If I can make jam, so can you. It's so satisfying. Get nice sweet hanepoot grapes for your jam. You don't have to cook pot after pot. Just cook enough for yourself. I'm giving the recipe for 1kg grapes. You can adjust it according to how many grapes you have. You'll need: …
html:
If I can make jam, so can you. It's so satisfying. Get nice sweet hanepoot grapes for your jam. You don't have to cook pot after pot. Just cook enough for yourself. I'm giving the recipe for 1kg grapes. You can adjust it according to how many grapes you have.

You'll need:

1kg grapes, picked off the stalk

750 g sugar

Juice of one lemon

30 ml vinegar

Method

Pick the grapes off the stem and wash them nice and clean.

Pack the grapes and sugar in layers in your pot and let it stand for 2 hours.

Start warming it slowly and stir until all the sugar has dissolved. The grapes shouldn't bubble and boil.

Once all the sugar has dissolved, add the lemon juice and vinegar. Now let it boil quickly over a higher heat.

Skim off the foam and pips. The vinegar helps the pips rise to the top.

When your jam reaches 105 °C, it's ready.

Bottle in sterilised jars while the jam is hot.

Enjoy with fresh, warm bread and snoek.

I sterilise my jars by boiling them in a pot of hot water on the stove. Be careful, the steam burns badly. Take them out of the pot with a pair of tongs and let them dry on a wire rack
